response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, hands-on senior engineer with nearly two decades of polyglot development experience, genuine low-latency distributed-systems work at Sociomantic, and recent exposure to AI-agent tooling (MCP code generators) at re:cinq. However, this is an executive search, and the resume shows no people-leadership, org-scaling, or executive experience whatsoever: every role is an individual-contributor position. There is no evidence of building or operating production multi-agent AI systems, no regulated-environment AI operations, no team scaling from 10 to 30+, and no governance of external delivery partners. The fintech exposure (RIDE Capital, ~15 months as a senior developer) is far short of the credit-risk and working-capital depth required. Strong IC profile, wrong role.
strengths: Deep hands-on backend experience, including low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic
Recent practical exposure to agentic-AI infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq
Broad polyglot range (TypeScript/Node, DLang, C/C++, Java) with strong testing and craftsmanship discipline
concerns: No senior technical-leadership or executive experience; no track record scaling engineering teams or developing technical leaders
No demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems experience — AI exposure is recent tooling contribution, not architecture ownership
No experience operating AI in regulated or high-stakes financial environments; fintech exposure is brief and IC-level

key_factors.factor: Absence of engineering leadership and org-scaling track record
No production multi-agent AI architecture experience
Strong distributed-systems and full-stack engineering depth
